channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: MultiGraSCCo: A Multilingual Anonymization Benchmark with Annotations of Personal Identifiers
item.description: arXiv:2603.08879v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accessing sensitive patient data for machine learning is challenging due to privacy concerns. Datasets with annotations of personally identifiable information are crucial for developing and testing anonymization systems to enable safe data sharing tha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, arxiv, study, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ConFu: Contemplate the Future for Better Speculative Sampling
item.description: arXiv:2603.08899v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding has emerged as a powerful approach to accelerate large language model (LLM) inference by employing lightweight draft models to propose candidate tokens that are subsequently verified by the target model. The effectiveness of this ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, prompt, framework, large language model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Automated Thematic Analysis for Clinical Qualitative Data: Iterative Codebook Refinement with Full Provenance
item.description: arXiv:2603.08989v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Thematic analysis (TA) is widely used in health research to extract patterns from patient interviews, yet manual TA faces challenges in scalability and reproducibility. LLM-based automation can help, but existing approaches produce codebooks with limi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, analysis, research, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning When to Sample: Confidence-Aware Self-Consistency for Efficient LLM Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.08999v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong reasoning performance through chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, yet often generate unnecessarily long reasoning paths that incur high inference cost. Recent self-consistency-based approaches further improve ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, large language model, arxiv, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reading, Not Thinking: Understanding and Bridging the Modality Gap When Text Becomes Pixels in Multimodal LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.09095v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can process text presented as images, yet they often perform worse than when the same content is provided as textual tokens. We systematically diagnose this "modality gap" by evaluating seven MLLMs across seven...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, multimodal, image, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bioalignment: Measuring and Improving LLM Disposition Toward Biological Systems for AI Safety
item.description: arXiv:2603.09154v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on internet-scale corpora can exhibit systematic biases that increase the probability of unwanted behavior. In this study, we examined potential biases towards synthetic vs. biological technological solutions acros...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, prompt, framework, release, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DEO: Training-Free Direct Embedding Optimization for Negation-Aware Retrieval
item.description: arXiv:2603.09185v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) have enabled diverse retrieval methods. However, existing retrieval methods often fail to accurately retrieve results for negation and exclusion queries. To addre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, embedding, multimodal, large language model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid adoption of LLM-based agentic systems has produced a rich ecosystem of frameworks (smolagents, LangGraph, AutoGen, CAMEL, LlamaIndex, i.a.). Yet existing benchmarks are model-centric: they fix the agentic setup and do not compare other syste...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, framework, arxiv, model, library | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LDP: An Identity-Aware Protocol for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08852v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As multi-agent AI systems grow in complexity, the protocols connecting them constrain their capabilities. Current protocols such as A2A and MCP do not expose model-level properties as first-class primitives, ignoring properties fundamental to effectiv...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, arxiv, model, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search
item.description: arXiv:2603.08877v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems combine iterative search, planning prompts, and retrieval backends, but deployed settings impose explicit budgets on tool calls and completion tokens. We present a controlled measurement study of ho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, prompt, RAG, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Interpretable Markov-Based Spatiotemporal Risk Surfaces for Missing-Child Search Planning with Reinforcement Learning and LLM-Based Quality Assurance
item.description: arXiv:2603.08933v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The first 72 hours of a missing-child investigation are critical for successful recovery. However, law enforcement agencies often face fragmented, unstructured data and a lack of dynamic, geospatial predictive tools. Our system, Guardian, provides an ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, release, RAG, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentOS: From Application Silos to a Natural Language-Driven Data Ecosystem
item.description: arXiv:2603.08938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid emergence of open-source, locally hosted intelligent agents marks a critical inflection point in human-computer interaction. Systems such as OpenClaw demonstrate that Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents can autonomously operate local com...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, large language model, RAG, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Consensus-Driven Multi-LLM Pipeline for Missing-Person Investigations
item.description: arXiv:2603.08954v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The first 72 hours of a missing-person investigation are critical for successful recovery. Guardian is an end-to-end system designed to support missing-child investigation and early search planning. This paper presents the Guardian LLM Pipeline, a mul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, fine-tuning, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Meissa: Multi-modal Medical Agentic Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2603.09018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-modal large language models (MM-LLMs) have shown strong performance in medical image understanding and clinical reasoning. Recent medical agent systems extend them with tool use and multi-agent collaboration, enabling complex decision-making. Ho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, reasoning, API, image, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MEMO: Memory-Augmented Model Context Optimization for Robust Multi-Turn Multi-Agent LLM Games
item.description: arXiv:2603.09022v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn, multi-agent LLM game evaluations often exhibit substantial run-to-run variance. In long-horizon interactions, small early deviations compound across turns and are amplified by multi-agent coupling. This biases win rate estimates and makes ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, prompt, framework, memory, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hindsight Credit Assignment for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.08754v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents often face significant credit assignment challenges in long-horizon, multi-step tasks due to sparse rewards. Existing value-free methods, such as Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), encounter two fundamental bo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, large language model, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-level meta-reinforcement learning with skill-based curriculum
item.description: arXiv:2603.08773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We consider problems in sequential decision making with natural multi-level structure, where sub-tasks are assembled together to accomplish complex goals. Systematically inferring and leveraging hierarchical structure has remained a longstanding chall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, product, framework, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Expressivity-Efficiency Tradeoffs for Hybrid Sequence Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.08859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hybrid sequence models--combining Transformer and state-space model layers--seek to gain the expressive versatility of attention as well as the computational efficiency of state-space model layers. Despite burgeoning interest in hybrid models, we lack...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, attention, memory, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, context, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, knowledge base, framework, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, framework, platform, vector, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: DuplexCascade: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech Dialogue with VAD-Free Cascaded ASR-LLM-TTS Pipeline and Micro-Turn Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.09180v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spoken dialog systems with cascaded ASR-LLM-TTS modules retain strong LLM intelligence, but VAD segmentation often forces half-duplex turns and brittle control. On the other hand, VAD-free end-to-end model support full-duplex interaction but is hard t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, arxiv, model, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The FABRIC Strategy for Verifying Neural Feedback Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08964v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forward reachability analysis is a dominant approach for verifying reach-avoid specifications in neural feedback systems, i.e., dynamical systems controlled by neural networks, and a number of directions have been proposed and studied. In contrast, fa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SPREAD: Subspace Representation Distillation for Lifelong Imitation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.08763v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key challenge in lifelong imitation learning (LIL) is enabling agents to acquire new skills from expert demonstrations while retaining prior knowledge. This requires preserving the low-dimensional manifolds and geometric structures that underlie tas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> demonstration, multimodal, framework, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Temporal Markov Transition Field
item.description: arXiv:2603.08803v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Markov Transition Field (MTF), introduced by Wang and Oates (2015), encodes a time series as a two-dimensional image by mapping each pair of time steps to the transition probability between their quantile states, estimated from a single global tra...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, RAG, arxiv, example, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SoftJAX & SoftTorch: Empowering Automatic Differentiation Libraries with Informative Gradients
item.description: arXiv:2603.08824v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automatic differentiation (AD) frameworks such as JAX and PyTorch have enabled gradient-based optimization for a wide range of scientific fields. Yet, many "hard" primitives in these libraries such as thresholding, Boolean logic, discrete indexing, an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, arxiv, study, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are Expressive Encoders Necessary for Discrete Graph Generation?
item.description: arXiv:2603.08825v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discrete graph generation has emerged as a powerful paradigm for modeling graph data, often relying on highly expressive neural backbones such as transformers or higher-order architectures. We revisit this design choice by introducing GenGNN, a modula...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, framework, arxiv, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: One Language, Two Scripts: Probing Script-Invariance in LLM Concept Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.08869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Do the features learned by Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) represent abstract meaning, or are they tied to how text is written? We investigate this question using Serbian digraphia as a controlled testbed: Serbian is written interchangeably in Latin and Cy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SciTaRC: Benchmarking QA on Scientific Tabular Data that Requires Language Reasoning and Complex Computation
item.description: arXiv:2603.08910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce SciTaRC, an expert-authored benchmark of questions about tabular data in scientific papers requiring both deep language reasoning and complex computation. We show that current state-of-the-art AI models fail on at least 23% of these quest...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, arxiv, model, analysis, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Equitable Multi-Task Learning for AI-RANs
item.description: arXiv:2603.08717v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-enabled Radio Access Networks (AI-RANs) are expected to serve heterogeneous users with time-varying learning tasks over shared edge resources. Ensuring equitable inference performance across these users requires adaptive and fair learning mechanism...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, experiment, arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A New Modeling to Feature Selection Based on the Fuzzy Rough Set Theory in Normal and Optimistic States on Hybrid Information Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Considering the high volume, wide variety, and rapid speed of data generation, investigating feature selection methods for big data presents various applications and advantages. By removing irrelevant and redundant features, feature selection reduces ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, API, memory, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, tool, example, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
